Politics.headline: Republican Louisiana senator Bill Cassidy loses primary after Trump intervenes to oust him
Politics.detail: Senator Bill Cassidy was defeated in the Republican primary in Louisiana after Donald Trump endorsed Julia Letlow. Cassidy had previously voted to convict Trump in his second impeachment trial.

Politics.headline: Federal funding for Trump's $400m ballroom in jeopardy after Senate ruling
Politics.detail: Funding for a proposed $400 million ballroom favored by President Trump is now in question following a recent Senate decision. The project's financial security is uncertain after a ruling within the Senate.

Politics.headline: 'Jim Crow 2.0': South Carolina's Republicans move to oust state's only Black congressman since 1897
Politics.detail: South Carolina Republicans are facing criticism for their efforts to remove the state's only Black congressman since 1897, sparking accusations of discriminatory practices. The situation is drawing comparisons to historical injustices.

Politics.headline: Trump Says He's a Builder. His Peace Deals Are Far From Sturdy.
Politics.detail: Arab officials are expressing concern that Trump might leave them in a difficult position regarding Iran, casting doubts on the long-term stability of his peace deals. Doubts are emerging about the durability of the peace agreements.

Politics.headline: Workers racing to turn reflecting pool blue for Trump may be at risk, union warns
Politics.detail: A union is raising concerns about the safety of workers who are rushing to paint a reflecting pool blue for Donald Trump. The workers' well-being may be in danger due to the accelerated work pace.

Finance & Economy.headline: Americans lean on credit cards and buy now, pay later as gas prices eat bigger share of income
Finance & Economy.detail: With rising gas prices, many Americans are increasingly relying on credit cards and 'buy now, pay later' services to manage their finances.

Finance & Economy.headline: Pound heads for worst week in 18 months as Burnham lines up Labour bid
Finance & Economy.detail: The British pound is on track for its worst week in 18 months amidst political uncertainty surrounding Burnham's potential leadership bid and rising inflation worries.

Finance & Economy.headline: Standard Bank uCount, FNB eBucks also boost fuel rewards
Finance & Economy.detail: Several banks in South Africa, including Standard Bank and FNB, are enhancing their fuel rewards programs to help customers cope with rising fuel costs.

Finance & Economy.headline: Americans face unexpected opportunity after housing market shift
Finance & Economy.detail: A shift in the housing market is creating an unexpected opportunity for some Americans.

Finance & Economy.headline: Walmart and Amazon compete for rural America with faster deliveries
Finance & Economy.detail: Walmart and Amazon are in competition to provide faster deliveries to rural areas of America, potentially tapping into a market worth up to $1 trillion annually.

Technology.headline: Google Nest Is Down, App Is Broken for Many Smart Home Users
Technology.detail: Reports began appearing on Downdetector around 3:30 a.m. ET indicating issues with Google Nest devices and app functionality.

Technology.headline: Microsoft adds more former Ai2 researchers, bolstering its Superintelligence team
Technology.detail: At least 10 former Allen Institute for AI staffers and researchers have joined Microsoft's Superintelligence team, including the core of the institute's OLMo open-source model effort, amid a shift in Ai2's funding toward applied uses of AI.

Technology.headline: Elon Musk's xAI Launches Grok Build, Its First AI Coding Agent
Technology.detail: An early beta is now available for SuperGrok Heavy subscribers, which starts at $300 a month.

Technology.headline: Pope creates artificial intelligence study group as Vatican prepares to release his first encyclical
Technology.detail: Pope Leo XIV has created a study group on artificial intelligence in a sign of his ongoing concern about the technology.

Technology.headline: Microsoft's Edge Drops Plaintext Password Loading After Backlash
Technology.detail: Microsoft has removed the plaintext password loading feature from its Edge browser following security concerns.

Science.headline: Scientists Reveal How Seven Days of Fasting Transforms the Human Body
Science.detail: Scientists have discovered that the human body undergoes a dramatic internal transformation during extended fasting, with major changes appearing only after about three days without food, according to a new study.

Science.headline: New nanotechnology treatment reverses Alzheimer's symptoms in mice
Science.detail: A new nanotechnology treatment reversed Alzheimer's symptoms in mice by restoring the brain's natural cleanup system; the specially engineered nanoparticles helped clear toxic amyloid proteins from the brain and repair the blood-brain barrier.

Science.headline: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda a global public health emergency
Science.detail: The World Health Organization declared an Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda a "public health emergency of international concern," noting that the outbreak is caused by the Bundibugyo virus.

Science.headline: Scientists Warn That the World's Rivers Are Running Out of Oxygen
Science.detail: A new study warns that the world’s rivers are running out of oxygen, which could have devastating consequences for aquatic life and human populations that depend on these rivers for drinking water and irrigation.

Science.headline: Neutrino flavor flips could be key to triggering supernovae
Science.detail: New research suggests that neutrino flavor flips might be the key to triggering supernovae.

Health.headline: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda a global health emergency
Health.detail: The World Health Organization has declared a new Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern, with at least 88 deaths reported. The outbreak is caused by the Bundibugyo virus, a rare strain of Ebola, and does not currently meet the criteria for a pandemic.

Health.headline: France says cruise ship Andes virus matches known South American viruses
Health.detail: French authorities have reported that a recent outbreak of Andes virus on a cruise ship matches known strains from South America, prompting increased vigilance and preventative measures. The virus, a type of hantavirus, can cause severe respiratory illness.

Health.headline: Scientists Discover Tiny Gut Particles That May Drive Aging and Chronic Disease
Health.detail: A new study indicates that microscopic particles from the gut may contribute to inflammation and chronic diseases related to aging. Gut particles from younger animals appeared to counteract these effects, suggesting potential therapeutic interventions.

Health.headline: US teens getting less sleep than ever, new report finds
Health.detail: A recent report highlights a concerning trend of decreasing sleep duration among teenagers in the United States, raising concerns about potential impacts on their health and well-being. Insufficient sleep has been linked to various health issues, including mental health problems and academic difficulties.

Health.headline: Is Cannabis Safe After 65? Stanford Experts Reveal 5 Risks Older Adults Should Know
Health.detail: As cannabis use increases among older adults, Stanford Medicine experts caution that today's marijuana is more potent, and the risks may be underestimated. They highlight five key risks that older adults should be aware of when considering cannabis use.

Sports.headline: Bryan Bagunas makes history with Japan SV League title
Sports.detail: Bryan Bagunas achieved a historic milestone by winning the Japan SV League title.

Sports.headline: Aaron Rodgers agrees to a 1-year deal to return to the Pittsburgh Steelers, AP sources say
Sports.detail: The four-time NFL MVP, Aaron Rodgers, has agreed to a one-year deal to return to the Pittsburgh Steelers.

Sports.headline: Ronda Rousey stops Gina Carano with an armbar 17 seconds into their double comeback bout
Sports.detail: Ronda Rousey defeated Gina Carano with an armbar just 17 seconds into their MMA comeback fight.

Sports.headline: Xabi Alonso hired by Chelsea on 4-year deal in return to coaching after Real Madrid disappointment
Sports.detail: Xabi Alonso has been appointed as Chelsea's manager on a four-year contract, marking his return to coaching after a period of disappointment with Real Madrid.

Sports.headline: Napoleon Solo holds off Iron Honor by 1 1/4 lengths to win the Preakness at Laurel Park
Sports.detail: Napoleon Solo emerged victorious in the Preakness Stakes at Laurel Park, defeating Iron Honor by a length and a quarter.

Entertainment.headline: Bulgaria wins Eurovision Song Contest clouded by politics and protests
Entertainment.detail: Bulgaria won the 70th Eurovision Song Contest with their upbeat party anthem 'Bangaranga', in a year where the field of 35 contestants was the smallest since 2003, and the event was marked by political tensions.

Entertainment.headline: MTV Splitsvilla X6: Gullu and Kaira crowned winners, take home Rs 20 lakh in 'Pyaar Ya Paisa' finale.
Entertainment.detail: Gullu and Kaira emerged as the winners of MTV Splitsvilla X6, taking home Rs 20 lakh in the 'Pyaar Ya Paisa' finale.

Entertainment.headline: Suriya's 'Karuppu' shows cancelled; RJ Balaji apologises to fans, says he is 'hopeful' about evening release.
Entertainment.detail: Shows of Suriya's 'Karuppu' were cancelled, and RJ Balaji apologized to fans, expressing hope for an evening release.

Entertainment.headline: Clarence Carter, US soul star who had hits with Patches and more, dies aged 90.
Entertainment.detail: Clarence Carter, the US soul singer known for hits like 'Patches', has passed away at the age of 90.

Entertainment.headline: Ronda Rousey Made Gina Carano Tap Out In 17 Seconds!!!
Entertainment.detail: Ronda Rousey defeated Gina Carano in just 17 seconds.

Crime & Justice.headline: Did a hitman kill a New York gallerist? Art world rocked by murder-for-hire trial
Crime & Justice.detail: Daniel Sikkema is accused of hiring someone to kill his husband, Brent Sikkema, amid a divorce and alleged fights over money.

Crime & Justice.headline: Shell-shocked: California man arrested for attempting to traffic wild turtles
Crime & Justice.detail: A California man was arrested for attempting to traffic wild turtles.

Crime & Justice.headline: At least five people pardoned by Trump for Capitol attack accused of new crimes
Crime & Justice.detail: Five individuals previously pardoned by Trump for their involvement in the Capitol attack are now facing new criminal allegations.

Crime & Justice.headline: Man accused of helping lead check fraud scheme pleads guilty in Michigan
Crime & Justice.detail: A man accused of playing a key role in a check fraud scheme has pleaded guilty in Michigan.

Crime & Justice.headline: Man shot dead while trying to kill his mom on Mother's Day, Southampton Police say
Crime & Justice.detail: Southampton Police reported that a man was fatally shot while attempting to kill his mother on Mother's Day.

Education.headline: Denver Public Schools celebrates teachers with first-ever 'Tag In' initiative during Appreciation Week
Education.detail: Denver Public Schools celebrated Teacher Appreciation Week 2026 with the "Teacher Tag In" initiative, where 180 volunteers covered school duties.

Education.headline: Adults locked out of learning to access education with new reform
Education.detail: A new reform aims to help adults who are locked out of learning to access education.

Education.headline: Early years initial teacher training (ITT) providers
Education.detail: Guidance has been released regarding early years initial teacher training (ITT) providers.

Education.headline: National Leaders of Governance for further education
Education.detail: Guidance has been issued for National Leaders of Governance for further education.

Education.headline: Frank Cottrell-Boyce on reading and schools accelerating screen use
Education.detail: Frank Cottrell-Boyce discusses reading and the increasing use of screens in schools.

Lifestyle.headline: Unhealthy lifestyle surging hypertension among youths alarmingly: Experts
Lifestyle.detail: Health experts in Bangladesh report that unhealthy lifestyles, poor diet, stress, lack of exercise, sleep disruption, and excessive screen time are alarmingly leading to hypertension, especially among youths, with one in five young people affected by high blood pressure. They also noted that the consumption of fruits and vegetables is declining, while smoking, drug use, air pollution, and noise pollution are emerging as major causes.

Lifestyle.headline: World Hypertension Day: Why some people get hypertension despite living a healthy lifestyle
Lifestyle.detail: A health expert explains that genetics, family history, and preventive genomics may influence blood pressure risk, with South Asians having a higher susceptibility to cardiometabolic disorders, including hypertension.  This means some individuals may react more strongly to moderate lifestyle risk factors, leading to earlier onset of the disease.

Lifestyle.headline: World Hypertension Day: 7 simple lifestyle changes that can help control blood pressure
Lifestyle.detail: World Hypertension Day highlights the dangers of high blood pressure, often called the 'silent killer' because it quietly damages the body for years.  The article suggests that hypertension is increasingly seen in younger adults due to obesity, stress, poor sleep, excess salt intake, and sedentary lifestyles and that lifestyle changes can significantly reduce BP and even delay the need for medicines.

Lifestyle.headline: Anne Lacheiner-Kuhn and the Art of Belonging
Lifestyle.detail: This article likely discusses Anne Lacheiner-Kuhn and her work related to the theme of belonging, potentially exploring her artistic expressions and perspectives on this concept. Further details would be available in the full article.

Lifestyle.headline: The do's and don'ts of having an enviable bed
Lifestyle.detail: Jolie Kerr shares expert tips on how to pick the right pillows, sheets, and mattress, and how to make your bed more inviting. The article emphasizes balancing comfort, practicality, and visual softness for a well-dressed bed.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Remove stumbling block in Sino-US ties: China Daily editorial
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The exchanges between the Chinese and US leaders have provided strategic guidance for the future of China-US relations, agreeing to build a 'constructive China-US relationship of strategic stability'. However, the editorial warns against moves that embolden 'Taiwan independence' separatist forces.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Opinion | Today's latest from Al Jazeera
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Al Jazeera presents a range of opinion pieces, including analysis of the strike that changed the geometry of war, the fertilizer crisis in Africa, and the challenges of discerning anti-Semitism from dissent in Britain.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Opinion: Could Metro-North face a strike?
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This article discusses the possibility of a Metro-North strike in the wake of a Long Island Rail Road strike, highlighting the disputes over wages, health costs, and work rules, and the potential disruption to commuters.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: The practical price of paid parental leave
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial discussing the tension between the benefits of longer paid parental leave and the financial feasibility for businesses, particularly in light of Allegheny County's proposal to mandate 18 weeks of paid leave.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Is France really poorer than Mississippi?
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Megan McArdle discusses the surprising statistic that Mississippi has a higher per capita GDP than France, exploring the implications for European living standards and the role of American innovation.

World.headline: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DRC, Uganda a global health emergency
World.detail: Health authorities have declared a global health emergency due to the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda, with the DRC accounting for most of the suspected cases. Over 80 deaths have been reported.

World.headline: At least four killed in Ukrainian drone attacks on Russia
World.detail: Ukrainian drone attacks on Russia have resulted in at least four deaths, with Russian defenses reportedly shooting down 81 drones headed for Moscow overnight. One of the deceased was an Indian worker.

World.headline: Nepal's Kami Rita Sherpa climbs Mount Everest for 32nd time, breaking his own record
World.detail: Kami Rita Sherpa, a mountaineer from Nepal, has successfully climbed Mount Everest for the 32nd time, surpassing his own previous record. This achievement underscores his remarkable expertise and endurance in mountaineering.

World.headline: India's Tata and Dutch giant ASML sign semiconductor deal during Modi visit
World.detail: During Prime Minister Modi's visit, India's Tata Group and Dutch company ASML have signed a semiconductor deal. Discussions also focused on enhancing cooperation in defense and security.

World.headline: Bulgaria wins Eurovision for first time; Israel comes second amid boycott
World.detail: Bulgaria has won the 70th Eurovision song contest for the first time with the song 'Bangaranga,' while Israel secured second place amidst boycott calls. The winning performer was Darina Nikolaeva Yotova, also known as Dara.

Zimbabwe.headline: Land allocation must be legal, orderly and accountable
Zimbabwe.detail: A call for legal and orderly land allocation practices in Zimbabwe, emphasizing accountability and proper procedures.

Zimbabwe.headline: PSMAS fraud case against Corporate 24 collapses
Zimbabwe.detail: A fraud case involving PSMAS and Corporate 24 has collapsed, potentially due to lack of evidence or procedural issues.

Zimbabwe.headline: Govt rolls out contingency food security plan . . . Probability of El Niño threat lingers
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zimbabwean government is implementing a contingency plan to ensure food security amid concerns about a possible El Niño weather pattern.

Zimbabwe.headline: RE: CHURCHES' SUBMISSION IN SUPPORT OF THE CONSTITUTION OF ZIMBABWE (AMENDMENT NO. 3) H.B.1, BILL, 2026
Zimbabwe.detail: A report on the submission by churches in support of the Constitution of Zimbabwe Amendment No. 3 Bill.

Zimbabwe.headline: 409 Farmers In Zimbabwe, It's All As Clear As Mud
Zimbabwe.detail: An analysis or commentary, possibly expressing confusion or uncertainty, regarding the situation of 409 farmers in Zimbabwe.
